prompt: I want to trace my DSPy pipeline and see what prompts and responses are being sent. How do I set up MLflow for this?
expected_output: Sets up mlflow.dspy.autolog() for auto-tracing with MLflow UI
assertions: Calls mlflow.dspy.autolog() before any DSPy code executes
Shows how to launch MLflow UI with mlflow ui command
Mentions what autolog captures: prompts, responses, token counts, latency
Includes pip install mlflow in setup

prompt: I want to compare different DSPy optimizers (BootstrapFewShot vs MIPROv2) and track which one gives the best results. How do I do experiment tracking with MLflow?
expected_output: Uses MLflow experiment tracking with separate runs for each optimizer
assertions: Calls mlflow.set_experiment() to group related runs
Uses mlflow.start_run() as a context manager for each optimizer run
Shows how to compare runs in the MLflow UI

prompt: I have an optimized DSPy program and want to version it so I can roll back if the new version performs worse in production. How do I use the MLflow model registry?
expected_output: Registers model with MLflow and uses aliases for production management
assertions: Uses mlflow.dspy.log_model() to log the DSPy module
Uses mlflow.register_model() to add to the registry
Uses model aliases (client.set_registered_model_alias) NOT the deprecated transition_model_version_stage
